description: metropolitan area in California, United States
description_wiki: The San Francisco Bay Area, commonly known as the Bay Area, is a region of California surrounding and including San Francisco Bay, and anchored by the cities of Oakland, San Francisco, and San Jose.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: public research university in Columbia, Missouri, USA
description_wiki: The University of Missouri (Mizzou or MU) is a public land-grant research university in Columbia, Missouri, United States.

description: local (U.S.) reporting initiative by The GroundTruth Project
description_wiki: Report for America (RFA) is a service program for emerging journalists in the United States.

description: nonprofit organization
description_wiki:
Teach for America (TFA) is an American nonprofit organization whose stated mission is to "enlist, develop, and mobilize as many as possible of our nation's most promising future leaders to grow and strengthen the movement for educational excellence."
The organization aims to accomplish this by recruiting and selecting college graduates from top universities around the United States to serve as teachers.

aliases: Corporation for National and Community Service
description: program of the U.S. federal government engaging adults in intensive community service
description_wiki: AmeriCorps ( ə-MERR-ih-kor; officially the Corporation for National and Community Service or CNCS) is an independent agency of the United States government that engages more than five million Americans in service through a variety of stipended volunteer work programs in many sectors.

description: county seat of Jefferson County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Louisville is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Kentucky, sixth-most populous city in the Southeast, and the 27th-most-populous city in the United States.

description_wiki: The Ohio River (Seneca: Ohi:yo') is a 981-mile-long (1,579 km) river in the United States.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description_wiki: The New York Times (NYT) is a newspaper based in Manhattan, New York City.

description: public university in Stony Brook, New York, United States
description_wiki: The State University of New York at Stony Brook, commonly referred to as Stony Brook University (SBU), is a public research university in Stony Brook, New York, United States, on Long Island.

entity: Alan Alda Center for Communicating Science

description: cross-disciplinary organization
description_wiki: The Alan Alda Center for Communicating Science is a cross-disciplinary organization founded in 2009 within Stony Brook University's School of Communication and Journalism, in Stony Brook, New York.
